reasoning: The informal statement asserts that a finite group G with |G| > 2 has no subgroup of order |G| - 1. The Lean formalization takes a finite group G with card G > 2, lets H be an arbitrary subgroup (implicitly universally quantified since H is a free parameter of the theorem), and asserts card H ≠ card G - 1. This is logically equivalent to "¬∃H, |H| = |G| - 1". The `Fintype G` assumption is implicit in the informal use of |G| (finite cardinality), and the `Fintype H` instance is automatically available for subgroups of a finite group. The types and quantifier structure correctly capture the claim.
